get.description: Returns all wagestep for a collective labour agreement based on function, level, type and education

schema.description: The unique number of the collective labour agreement, as returned from the /get-collective-labour-agreements endpoint.
The functionLevel, as returned from the /get-functions endpoint.
The function type, as returned from the /get-functions endpoint.
The functionSubtype
The education, the name as returned from the /get-educations endpoint.

parameters.name: effectiveDate
parameters.description:
The effective date for which the wage applies, in YYYY-MM-DD format.
Defaults to today's date if not provided.
Is adjusted forward to today, if a date in the past is provided. This ensures that an active wage set is used
(older wages sets are expired, and are then no longer used).

For example, say that today is 2026-01-02, say there was a wage increase on 2026-01-01, and say you call this
endpoint with an effectiveDate of 2025-12-31. This endpoint will then implicitly act as if you called it with an
effectiveDate of 2026-01-02. That is, it will use the wage set of 2026-01-01.

This means that if you called it on 2025-12-31 and then call it again on 2026-01-01, it will return a different
result for the same input parameters, because the effective date is silently increased to 2026-01-01.

This adjustment is done implicitly for backwards compatibility reasons. Future HTTP APIs may instead of
return an HTTP 400 with an error code instead.

400.description:

403.description: Forbidden. User is not authorized to access data.

get.summary: Determines the hourly wage for a function
get.description:
Determines the minimum hourly wage for a function.
This depends on the chosen fields through the other endpoints in this API.
If a field has not been chosen, the highest applicable option will be chosen automatically.

schema.description: The unique number of the collective labour agreement, as returned from the /get-collective-labour-agreements endpoint.
The functionLevel, as returned from the /get-functions endpoint.
The function type, as returned from the /get-functions endpoint.
The functionSubtype
The education, the name as returned from the /get-educations endpoint.
The wageStep, as returned from the /get-wage-steps endpoint.
Forces using high AWf premium. If omitted or set to false, the system determines the applicable AWf premium automatically.

post.summary: Set the start and end date of an existing employment contract
post.description:
Set the start and end date of an existing employment contract. When the end date is not provided, it's
automatically calculated based on the provided start date and the duration that was specified when creating the
employment contract. This step is necessary before creating placements for this employment contract. It's not
possible to change the dates of an employment contract when they are already set

schema.description: The unique id of the worker, as returned from the /create-worker endpoint.

post.summary: Pre check for a placement creation
post.description:
Performs a pre-check to determine whether a placement can be created with the provided data.
Note: a successful pre-check does not guarantee that the placement can still be created later.
For example, the minimum CLA wage may have changed between the pre-check and the actual creation of the placement.

200.description: Successful pre-check with a possible error.

403.description: The data that the request referred to is inaccessible to the caller.

post.summary: Create a placement for a worker, to work at a client company
post.description:
Create a placement for a worker. This includes the creation of an employment contract (if no active contract
exists). The contract is auto-signed, so a signed framework agreement (see /framework-agreement) must be present
The created contract has a duration of four weeks. This can be shortened: see /change-end-date. if the client
company invokes the agency clause (Dutch: uitzendbeding).

post.summary: Create a new entitlement registration line
post.description:
Creates a new entitlement registration line with an optional file attachment.

**Attachment Rules:**
- Whether an attachment is required or not allowed is determined by the entitlement configuration.
- entitlements API can be used to fetch the entitlement configuration, which includes whether an attachment is required or not allowed for a specific entitlement.
- If an attachment is required but not provided, the request will be rejected with a 400 error.
- If an attachment is provided when not configured in entitlement, the document will still be stored
- Supported file types for attachments are PDF, PNG and JPEG. If the provided file is not one of these types, the request will be rejected with a 400 error.

requestBody.description: Entitlement registration line data with optional PDF attachment

request.description:
JSON object with the registration data.
See schema: [EntitlementRegistrationRequestV1](#model-EntitlementRegistrationRequestV1)

attachment.description:
PDF or image file attachment (receipt, invoice, etc.).
Whether required or not is determined by the entitlement configuration.

400.description:
The request could not be processed. This could be due to a number of reasons:
* The request does not have all required data.
* Data in the request is invalid.
* Data in the request is inconsistent.
* The operation is not allowed given the current state of the data.
* The entitlement requires an attachment but none was provided.

401.description: The request did not contain credentials identifying the caller, or the provided credentials were invalid.

403.description:
The caller does not have permission to perform this action.
This could be due to:
* Lacking permission to access the placement's company or worker.
* The company or worker being blacklisted for time writing.

post.summary: Retrieve applicable surcharges for a registration period
post.description:
This endpoint calculates and returns applicable surcharges, based on the provided CAO number, function type, and registration periods.

Note: In case a CLA has set both a fixed amount and a factor for a surcharge and stacking rule higher, this endpoint will arbitrarily choose the factor-based surcharge.

400.description:

post.summary: Invite a worker to join Maqqie
post.description:
Invite a worker to join Maqqie. If freelance properties are provided, the API will also create a freelance company for the worker.
This is intended for company users to onboard workers.

400.description:
The request could not be processed. Possible error codes:
* `INVALID_EMAIL` - The email address format is invalid.
* `INVALID_PHONE_NUMBER` - The mobile number format is invalid or not supported.
* `INVALID_ZIP_CODE` - The zip code format is invalid.
* `INVALID_IBAN` - The IBAN format is invalid.
* `EMAIL_ADDRESS_IN_USE` - A worker with this email address already exists for this company.

401.description: The request did not contain credentials identifying the caller, or the provided credentials were invalid.

* The request does not have all required data.
* Data in the request is invalid.
* Data in the request is inconsistent.
* The operation is not allowed given the current state of the data.

401.description: The request did not contain credentials identifying the caller, or the provided credentials were invalid.

403.description: The data that the request referred to is inaccessible to the caller.

companyId.description: The unique id of the client company.

contactId.description: The unique id of the client company's contact person on whose behalf this API is called.

workerPointOfContactContactId.description: The unique id of the client company's contact person that acts as the point of contact for the worker. Their phone number will be shared with the worker.

date.description:
The date, that is considered to be the date of drafting the arbo document.
In ISO 8601 calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD).
TODO: check whether there are any restrictions on the date.

functionId.description:
The function applicable to the activities for the work that this arbo document is valid for (arbo documents
cannot apply to more than one function).
The id must be a value returned from the collective-labour-agreement API.
The function's collective labour agreement must be one of the client company's collective labour agreements.

isDocumentForSafetyEducation.description:
`true` if there is a document with general safety-related information material, `false` otherwise.
The caller of this endpoint should make that document available together with the created arbo document.

isDocumentForSpecificSafetyRegulations.description:
`true` if there is a document with client-company safety precautions for this arbo document's function, `false` otherwise.
The caller of this endpoint should make that document available together with the created arbo document.

isDocumentForInHouseEmergencyServiceAgre.description:
`true` if there is a document with client-company arrangements for emergency response, `false` otherwise.
The caller of this endpoint should make that document available together with the created arbo document.

isSafetyProtectedEquipment.description:
`true` if the worker needs equipment for safety protection, `false` otherwise.
If true, it is mandatory for the client company to provide that equipment (in a timely manner).

isWorkOnThirdPartyLocations.description:
`true` if the worker will work at third-party locations (i.e. locations not belonging to the client company).
If true, the client company is responsible for providing any necessary information about safety and health
regulations at those locations.

fileId.description: The unique id of the arbo document PDF.

minimumBaseHourlyWage.description: The minimum wage per hour, in €.

minimumHourlyWageIncludingReservations.description: The minimum wage per hour, including reservations for holiday payment, leave days and public holidays in €.

costPrice.description: The cost price (factor) per hour, including reservations, insurances and premiums in € (excluding provision)

collectiveLabourAgreementNumber.description: number for the collective labour agreement as published by Centraal Bureau voor de Statistiek

functionLevels.description: The possible function levels for this function, can be one or more

functionTypes.description: The possible function types for this function, can be empty, one or more

functionNatures.description: The possible function natures for this function, can be one or more

vatNumber.description: The VAT number of the company. Must be valid according to https://ec.europa.eu/taxation_customs/vies

collectiveLabourAgreementNumber.description: The unique number of the collective labour agreement, as returned from the /get-collective-labour-agreements endpoint.

ProvisionV1.description:
The fixedCostPriceFactor determines the cost price (hourly wage * fixedCostPriceFactor = cost price).
If no fixed cost price should be used this field should not be send

The percentage and minimumAmount are applied on top of the cost price, where the highest of the two is invoiced.
If the cost price is 20€, percentage is 5% and minimum amount is 0.50€, then the client will be invoiced for 21€ (5% of 20€ = 1€ > 0.50€)
If the cost price is 20€, percentage is 5% and minimum amount is 2.50€, then the client will be invoiced for 22.50€ (2.50€ > 5% of 20€ = 1€)
If only one of them is applicable, the other one can be set to 0.0

ContactCreationRequestV1.description: Request payload for creating a new contact. The companyId field specifies the company to which the new contact belongs.

CompanyCreationErrorV1.description:
Error codes for company creation. We currently only support one company per chamber of commerce number through the API. Phone number errors:
* The phone number does not start with a country code. It must begin with either a '+' or '00' followed by the country code
* The phone number cannot be parsed. This means the format of the phone number is invalid and cannot be interpreted as a valid phone number
* The phone number type is not supported. This includes phone numbers that are not suitable for the application's use cases, such as voicemail access numbers
* The phone number violates country-specific rules and cannot exist in practice. Examples include phone numbers that are too long for their country or Dutch cell phone numbers starting with +3169, which are not valid

LocationCreationErrorV1.description:
Error codes for location creation. Name must be unique per company. Phone number errors:
* The phone number does not start with a country code. It must begin with either a '+' or '00' followed by the country code
* The phone number cannot be parsed. This means the format of the phone number is invalid and cannot be interpreted as a valid phone number
* The phone number type is not supported. This includes phone numbers that are not suitable for the application's use cases, such as voicemail access numbers
* The phone number violates country-specific rules and cannot exist in practice. Examples include phone numbers that are too long for their country or Dutch cell phone numbers starting with +3169, which are not valid

LocationUpdateErrorV1.description:
Error codes for location update. Phone number errors:
* The phone number does not start with a country code. It must begin with either a '+' or '00' followed by the country code
* The phone number cannot be parsed. This means the format of the phone number is invalid and cannot be interpreted as a valid phone number
* The phone number type is not supported. This includes phone numbers that are not suitable for the application's use cases, such as voicemail access numbers
* The phone number violates country-specific rules and cannot exist in practice. Examples include phone numbers that are too long for their country or Dutch cell phone numbers starting with +3169, which are not valid

ContactCreationErrorV1.description:
Error codes for contact creation. Email address must be unique per company. Phone number errors:
* The phone number does not start with a country code. It must begin with either a '+' or '00' followed by the country code
* The phone number cannot be parsed. This means the format of the phone number is invalid and cannot be interpreted as a valid phone number
* The phone number type is not supported. This includes phone numbers that are not suitable for the application's use cases, such as voicemail access numbers
* The phone number violates country-specific rules and cannot exist in practice. Examples include phone numbers that are too long for their country or Dutch cell phone numbers starting with +3169, which are not valid

ContactUpdateErrorV1.description:
Error codes for contact update. Phone number errors:
* The phone number does not start with a country code. It must begin with either a '+' or '00' followed by the country code.
* The phone number cannot be parsed. This means the format of the phone number is invalid and cannot be interpreted as a valid phone number.
* The phone number type is not supported. This includes phone numbers that are not suitable for the application's use cases, such as voicemail access numbers.
* The phone number violates country-specific rules and cannot exist in practice. Examples include phone numbers that are too long for their country or Dutch cell phone numbers starting with +3169, which are not valid.

street.description: The street name of the address.

houseNumber.description: The house number of the address, excluding suffix. For a house number 123A, this would be 123.

houseNumberAddition.description: House number suffix (if any). For a house number 123A, this would be 'A'.

zipCode.description: The postal code of the address. Must be a post code that actually exists in the Netherlands.

CountryCode.description: The country code in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format.

EmploymentContractStatusV1.description: The status of the employment contract

EmploymentContractTypeV1.description: The type of the employment contract

EmploymentContractDurationTypeV1.description: The type of how the duration of the employment contract is specified

durationInWeeks.description: The duration of the employment contract in weeks. Is required when type is WEEKS_STARTING_AFTER_FRAMEWORK_AGREEMENT.

startDate.description: The start date of the employment contract. Is required when type is START_END_DATE or START_DATE_INDEFINITE_END_DATE.

endDate.description: The end date of the employment contract. Is required when duration type is START_END_DATE.

workerId.description: The unique id of the worker, as returned from the /create-worker endpoint.

hoursPerWeek.description:
The number of hours the worker will work per week for this employment contract.
The maximum comes from https://www.rijksoverheid.nl/onderwerpen/werktijden/vraag-en-antwoord/wettelijke-regels-werktijden-en-rusttijden

isPayrollTaxReduction.description: Whether the payroll tax reduction applies to this employment contract.

EmploymentContractCreationRequestV1.description: Under construction. Lots of additional properties will be added in the future.

employmentContractId.description: The unique id of the created employment contract.

signingUrl.description: A URL to sign the employment contract

signingUrlExpirationTimestamp.description: The timestamp (UTC) at which the signing URL will expire.

message.description: A human-readable message that provides context to the error

employmentContractId.description: The unique id of the employment contract.

startDate.description: The new start date of the employment contract.

endDate.description:
The new end date of the employment contract. When not provided, it will be calculated based on the provided
start date and the duration specified when creating the employment contract.

message.description: A human-readable message that provides context to the error

employmentContractId.description: The unique id of the employment contract.

hoursPerWeek.description: The number of hours the worker will work per week for this employment contract.

isPayrollTaxReduction.description: Whether the payroll tax reduction applies to this employment contract.

startDate.description: The start date of the employment contract.

endDate.description: The end date of the employment contract. The contract runs up to and including this date.

signingUrl.description: A URL to sign the employment contract. This is only provided when the contract is in PENDING_SIGNATURE status.

signingUrlExpirationTime.description: The timestamp (UTC) at which the signing URL will expire. This is only provided when the contract is in PENDING_SIGNATURE status.

invoiceLabel.description: The wage compensation subtype, shown as a label on the invoice and payslip (e.g. "Declaratie").

fixedAmountInEuros.description:
Null for entitlements that do not have a pre-defined amount of €.
If this is null, you can (and must) specify an amount in € when registering on this entitlement, i.e. when calling the  Registration API

requireSupportingDocument.description: Whether a supporting document (e.g. receipt) must be attached when registering.

effectiveFrom.description: The date from which this entitlement is effective.

effectiveUntilInclusive.description: The date until which this entitlement is effective, inclusive. Null if the entitlement has no end date.

project.description: The project this entitlement is scoped to. Null if the entitlement applies to all projects.

CompensationType.description: Whether this entitlement is a gross or net type.

FrameworkAgreementGetResponseV1.description:
If there is a signed agreement, `unsignedAgreement` is `null`.
If there is no signed agreement, but there is an unsigned agreement, `signedAgreement` is `null`.
If neither exists, both are `null`.

signingUrl.description: A URL to sign the framework agreement.

signingUrlExpirationTimestamp.description: The timestamp (UTC) at which the signing URL will expire.

documentId.description:
The id of the document containing the signed framework agreement.
The document can be retrieved using the /worker-document endpoint.

FrameworkAgreementSignedEventV1.description: Request body for the webhook that is called once a worker has signed the framework agreement.

workerId.description: The unique id of the worker, as returned from the /create-worker endpoint.

isPayrollTaxReduction.description: true if the worker wants us to apply payroll tax reduction (Dutch: loonheffingskorting), false otherwise

purchaseOrderNumber.description: The purchase order number to add to the invoice. Maximum length is 12 characters.

workerId.description: The unique id of the worker, as returned from the /create-worker endpoint.

companyId.description: The unique id of the client company.

departmentId.description:
Optional. The unique id of the client company's department at which the worker will work.
If not provided, the first department of the client company is used.

locationId.description: The unique id of the client company's location at which the worker will work.

contactId.description: The unique id of the client company's contact person (who will be mentioned in the contract).

startDate.description: The start date of the placement's contract, in ISO 8601 calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD).

endDate.description: The end date of the placement's contract, in ISO 8601 calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD).

shiftStartDateTime.description: The timestamp when the shift connected to the placement starts. Format: ISO 8601, must be in UTC. example: '2024-09-25T15:30:00Z'

shiftEndDateTime.description: The timestamp when the shift connected to the placement ends. Format: ISO 8601, must be in UTC. example: '2024-09-25T18:30:00Z'

collectiveLabourAgreementNumber.description:
Number for this placement's collective labour agreement, as published by Centraal Bureau voor de Statistiek.
Must be one of the collective labour agreements that are already registered for the given companyId

functionId.description:
The function applicable to the worker's activities for this placement.
The id must be a value returned from the collective-labour-agreement API.
Must be compatible with the collectiveLabourAgreementNumber.

functionType.description:
The function type applicable to the worker's activities for this placement.
Must be a value returned from the collective-labour-agreement API.
Must be compatible with the functionId.

functionLevel.description:
The function level applicable to the worker's activities for this placement.
Must be a value returned from the collective-labour-agreement API.
Must be compatible with the functionId.

education.description:
The education level applicable to the worker's activities for this placement.
Must be a value returned from the collective-labour-agreement API.
Must be compatible with the collectiveLabourAgreementNumber, functionType, functionLevel, and worker's age.

wageStep.description:
The wage step applicable to the worker for this placement.
Must be a value returned from the collective-labour-agreement API.
Must be compatible with the collectiveLabourAgreementNumber, functionType, functionLevel, worker's age, education, and placement start date.

baseHourlyWage.description:
The hourly wage the worker will earn for this placement, excluding surcharges, holiday allowance, etc.
Must be compatible with the collective labour agreement-related properties, such as functionLevel, education, and wageStep.

hoursPerWeek.description:
The number of hours the worker will work per week for this placement.
The maximum comes from https://www.rijksoverheid.nl/onderwerpen/werktijden/vraag-en-antwoord/wettelijke-regels-werktijden-en-rusttijden

The new end date of the placement's contract. The contract runs up to and including this date.
Must be >= contracted placement start date, and < contracted placement end date.

placementId.description: The unique id of the placement.

BillingProfileV1.description:
The billing profile applicable to the worker for this placement.
All placements with the same billingProfile will be on a separate invoice.
When empty, all registrations of all placements will end up on the same invoice.

PreCheckPlacementCreationResponse.description:
Successful pre-check with a possible error. If no error is present the placement is eligible for creation.
Note: this does not guarantee that the placement can still be created later.
For example, the minimum CLA wage may have changed between the pre-check and the actual creation of the placement.

message.description: A human-readable message that provides context to the error
message.example: Base hourly wage (10.15) is below the minimum (13.25, based on the CLA)

registrationId.description: Unique identifier of a registration. A single registration is associated with 1 or more registration lines, including normal hours, overtime and surcharges

placementId.description: The UUID of the placement for which hours are being registered.

projectId.description: The UUID of the project if the hours are registered on a project.

startTime.description: The timestamp when the work period started. Format: ISO 8601, must be in UTC. example: '2024-09-25T15:30:00Z'

endTime.description: The timestamp when the work period ended. Format: ISO 8601 (e.g., 2024-09-25T15:30:00Z). Expected in UTC.

breakDuration.description: The optional, combined duration of breaks taken during the work period. Format: ISO 8601 (e.g., PT1H30M).

description.description: An optional description or notes about the work period.

placementId.description: The UUID of the placement for which the kilometer allowance is being registered.

projectId.description: The UUID of the project if the kilometer allowance is registered on a project.

date.description: The date of the trip. Format: ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD)

KilometerAllowanceRegistrationRequestV1.description:
If toAddress is empty, the address of the location of the placement will be used.
If fromAddress is empty, the home address of the worker will be used.

kilometers.description: The total distance registered, in kilometers. This includes the return trip, if applicable.

amount.description: The wage for the kilometers, in €.

HoursRegistrationErrorV1.description: Error codes related to the submission of hours.

placementId.description: The UUID of the placement for which the entitlement is being registered.

entitlementId.description:
The UUID of the entitlement.
The list of registrable entitlements for a placement can be fetched from entitlements API

amountInEuros.description:
The registration amount in euros (€),
Only used when the entitlement is not fixed-amount based.
If the entitlement is fixed-amount based, this value will be ignored and the value stored while creating the entitlement wil be used

date.description: The date on which the entitlement took place. Format: ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD)

description.description: Optional description or comment about this registration

EntitlementRegistrationRequestV1.description: Request body for creating an entitlement registration line

registrationId.description: The ID of the created entitlement registration line

attachmentFileId.description: The ID of the uploaded attachment file. Null if no attachment was uploaded.

EntitlementRegistrationResponseV1.description: Response after saving an entitlement registration line

message.description: A human-readable message that provides context to the error

EntitlementRegistrationErrorV1.description: Error response for entitlement registration failures

EntitlementRegistrationErrorCodeV1.description:
Error codes for entitlement registration failures:
- `WRONG_ATTACHMENT_FILE_TYPE`: The uploaded file is not a supported type (PDF, PNG, or JPEG).
- `ATTACHMENT_REQUIRED`: The entitlement requires a supporting document but none was provided.
- `FILE_SIZE_EXCEEDS_LIMIT`: The uploaded file exceeds the maximum allowed size.
- `ENTITLEMENT_NOT_FOUND`: No entitlement exists for the given entitlementId.
- `ENTITLEMENT_NOT_EFFECTIVE_ON_DATE`: The registration date falls outside the entitlement's effective period.
- `AMOUNT_MISSING_FOR_ENTITLEMENT`: In case of Non-fixed entitlement requires amountInEuros to be provided.
- `INVALID_DATA`: Generic error code; details will be in message.

caoNumber.description: The CAO number relevant to the registration.

companyId.description: The UUID of the company of the registration.

functionType.description: The function type relevant to the registration.

functionSubType.description: The function sub type relevant to the registration.

startDateTime.description: Start date and time of the registration period in UTC. Must be before endDateTime.

endDateTime.description: End date and time of the registration period in UTC. Must be after startDateTime.

RegistrationPeriodV1.description: Registration period to calculate surcharges for.

surcharges.description: Array of applicable surcharges for the registration period.

surchargeFactor.description: Factor to be applied as a surcharge (percentage-based).

fixedSurchargeAmountPerHour.description: Fixed amount per hour to be applied as a surcharge (euro amount).

startDateTime.description: Start date and time for the surcharge period in UTC.

endDateTime.description: End date and time for the surcharge period in UTC.

SurchargeResponseErrorV1.description: Error codes and messages for surcharge retrieval error.

mobilePhoneNumber.description:
The mobile number of the worker.
* Must include a country code (e.g. +31 or 0031).
* Must be a number that can exist in practice.
  Examples of numbers that **cannot** exist include phone numbers that are too long for their country or Dutch cell phone numbers starting with +3169.
* The phone number type must be supported. This excludes phone numbers that are not suitable for the application's use cases, such as voicemail access numbers.
* The phone number must be parsable. This means the format of the phone number is valid and can be be interpreted as a valid phone number.

firstName.description: The first name of the worker.

emailAddress.description: The email address of the worker.

mobilePhoneNumber.description: The mobile number of the worker.
The mobile number of the worker.

firstNameFormal.description: The formal first name of the worker.

middleName.description: The middle name of the worker.

lastName.description: The last name of the worker.

dateOfBirth.description: The date of birth of the worker.

placeOfBirth.description: The place of birth of the worker.

identificationBy.description: The method used for worker identification.

idCheckDate.description: The date when the identification document was checked.

bsnDocument.description: The base64-encoded image of the bsn document.

base64DocumentBack.description: The base64-encoded image of the back of the identity document.

base64DocumentFront.description: The base64-encoded image of the front of the identity document.

documentExpiryDate.description: The expiration date of the identification document.

legalWorkCategory.description: The legal work categories that are currently supported.

rightToWorkUntil.description: The date when the workers right to work expires. This is not inclusive.

NonEerWorkCategory.description: This object contains the legal work category of the worker for non-EER residents. This is only required for non-EER residents.

rightToWorkUntil.description: The date when the workers right to work expires. This is not inclusive.

bsn.description: The BSN (Burgerservicenummer). If provided, updates the employee's BSN if not already set.

emailAddress.description: The email address of the worker.

firstName.description: The first name of the worker.

lastName.description: The last name of the worker.

shouldNotifyWorkerOfAccountCreation.description: Whether to send an invitation email and SMS to the worker.

mobileNumber.description:
The mobile number of the worker.
* Must include a country code (e.g. +31 or 0031).
* Must be a number that can exist in practice.
  Examples of numbers that **cannot** exist include phone numbers that are too long for their country or Dutch cell phone numbers starting with +3169.
* The phone number type must be supported. This excludes phone numbers that are not suitable for the application's use cases, such as voicemail access numbers.
* The phone number must be parsable. This means the format of the phone number is valid and can be be interpreted as a valid phone number.

city.description: The city where the worker resides.

dateOfBirth.description: The date of birth of the worker.

houseNumber.description: The house number of the worker.

houseNumberAddition.description: The house number addition of the worker.

workerId.description: The external ID of the created worker.

schema.description: The unique number of the collective labour agreement, as returned from the /get-collective-labour-agreements endpoint.
The function type, as returned from the /get-functions endpoint.
The functionLevel, as returned from the /get-functions endpoint.
The functionSubtype
The education, the name as returned from the /get-educations endpoint.
The wageStep, as returned from the /get-wage-steps endpoint.
Forces using high AWf premium. If omitted or set to false, the system determines the applicable AWf premium automatically.

effectiveDate.name: effectiveDate
effectiveDate.description:
The effective date for which the wage applies, in YYYY-MM-DD format.
Defaults to today's date if not provided.
Is adjusted forward to today, if a date in the past is provided. This ensures that an active wage set is used
(older wages sets are expired, and are then no longer used).

For example, say that today is 2026-01-02, say there was a wage increase on 2026-01-01, and say you call this
endpoint with an effectiveDate of 2025-12-31. This endpoint will then implicitly act as if you called it with an
effectiveDate of 2026-01-02. That is, it will use the wage set of 2026-01-01.

This means that if you called it on 2025-12-31 and then call it again on 2026-01-01, it will return a different
result for the same input parameters, because the effective date is silently increased to 2026-01-01.

This adjustment is done implicitly for backwards compatibility reasons. Future HTTP APIs may instead of
return an HTTP 400 with an error code instead.

schema.description: The wage per hour, in €.
The unique id of the client company.
The unique id of the worker, as returned from the /create-worker endpoint.
The unique id of the employment contract.
